home.lede: Discovery & verification controller for the Vortex peer-to-peer network.
home.fingerprintDesc: Pin this public key in your Vortex client. Every response from the controller is signed with it — a mismatch means someone is tampering.
home.step2: Pick the Global network mode in the setup wizard.
home.step3: Paste the controller URL (auto-resolved via SNS) or an entry URL from this site.
home.step4: Verify the pubkey in your app matches the fingerprint above.
home.step5: You're connected. Everything else (node list, signatures) is handled automatically.
home.nodesDesc: All registered peers, their weights, endpoints, and verification sources.
home.entriesDesc: Bootstrap URLs for first-time clients — Cloudflare tunnels, Tor, IPFS, direct.
home.mirrorsDesc: Alternative hosts serving this same signed content in case of censorship.
home.securityDesc: Full breakdown of what's cryptographically verified, what's not, and why.

nodes.title: Registered nodes
nodes.lede: Every peer the controller knows about. Clients read this list, verify the signature, and pick a node to connect to based on weight. A fresh sealed node gets weight 1.0; stale or unsealed nodes decay toward 0 so traffic flows to live, trusted peers only.
nodes.legendDualD: Registered with the controller AND sealed on-chain via Solana. Two independent witnesses confirm the code hash.
nodes.legendCtrlD: Registered with the HTTP controller. Signature proves the node owns its private key, but code integrity is not anchored on-chain.
nodes.legendUnverifD: Known from a config file or gossip. Not yet authenticated. Treat with caution — anyone can advertise a pubkey until proof arrives.

entries.title: Entry URLs
entries.lede: Starting points for a client that has no peers yet. Each entry URL is a way to reach the controller — through a Cloudflare tunnel, Tor, IPFS gateway, or a direct domain. Copy any one into your Vortex app on first launch.
entries.typeTunnelD: Free *.trycloudflare.com address. No account required. URL changes when the tunnel restarts — treat as ephemeral.
entries.typeTorD: .onion address, reachable only through Tor. Censor-proof, slower. Needs a Tor client or bridge.
entries.typeIpfsD: Content-addressed copy of the static frontend. Served from any IPFS gateway — no single server to block.
entries.typeDirectD: Regular domain + TLS. Fastest but blockable at the DNS/IP level.

mirrors.title: Mirror sites
mirrors.lede: Alternative places that serve the exact same signed content as this site. Useful if vortexx.sol is blocked at your DNS/ISP. Each mirror is periodically checked — the colored dot reflects the last probe result.
mirrors.healthyD: Probed within the last 5 minutes and responded OK. Safe to use right now. Latency is shown on the card.
mirrors.unreachableD: Last probe failed — connection refused, timeout, 5xx. The mirror may be down, blocked, or overloaded. Try the next one.
mirrors.noprobeD: Tor .onion mirrors can't be health-checked without a local Tor proxy. Status is unknown — use a Tor browser to test.

security.title: Security model
security.lede: Exactly what's cryptographically verified, what's not, and how each key in the system earns — or loses — your trust. Read this before you connect new clients to a controller you don't run yourself.
